name: Tenth Planet Jiu Jitsu Newton
logoDescription: Official 10th Planet globe mark — flame/amber on the left hemisphere, electric cyan on the right — with gold 'PLANET' wordmark (red letter outlines), white 'JIU JITSU', and royal blue 'NEWTON' containing a red crescent. Fire-vs-ice duality.
designStyleNote: Base structure from cinematic-fight (obsidian substrate, oversized display type, prestige-TV feel) BUT swap the classic oxblood accent for the logo's fire-orange + ice-cyan dual accent system. Use warm off-white body copy for legibility. Colors are used sparingly — as moments, not wallpaper.
usageRules: Default substrate is near-black (#0A0A0B). 90%+ of every page should be this plus the carbon/steel tints — NOT wall-to-wall orange or cyan.
Flame orange (#FF6A14) is the PRIMARY action color — CTAs, hero emphasis word, underline on hover, the one thing the eye chases. Never use it as a section background.
Ice cyan (#1BA8E8) is the SECONDARY accent — used sparingly for links, highlight moments, and one cold counterpoint per section. Think opposite-corner balance with flame.
Gold (#F5B015) is reserved for the one-of-a-kind moments — logo-adjacent treatments, premium callouts, champion/competition class badges. Under 5% of any page.
Fire + ice may appear in the same composition (hero gradient, program cards, divider glyphs) but should never fight for the same eye-path. Spatially separate them.
NEVER use red (#B82418) and flame orange together in close proximity — the logo already balances them for you. Use red only for rare danger/crimson moments or a single letter-outline tribute.
Body text is warm bone (#D8D2C4), NOT pure white. Pure white feels clinical against this brand.
Gradients allowed: flame → ember (warm), ice → royal (cold), or a diagonal flame-to-ice for ONE hero-scale moment per site.
Film grain and subtle noise overlays are encouraged on hero/full-bleed sections — keeps the cinematic-fight feel without relying on saturation.

displayFont.usage: Oversized hero headlines, section intros, program names — condensed/compressed display look that echoes fight-poster type
displayFont.transform: UPPERCASE for display, Title Case for subheads

bodyFont.usage: All body copy, paragraphs, nav, forms, meta
bodyFont.tracking: 0 for body, +0.04em for small caps and eyebrows

accentFont.usage: Stat blocks, big numbers, weight classes, round timers — mechanical display type

motifs: The globe — spherical divider motif, circular crop on portraits, orbit-path lines between program cards
Flame streaks — diagonal gradient washes from #FF6A14 → transparent on hero edges
Electric arcs — thin cyan lines as section dividers (1px solid #1BA8E8 at 30% opacity)
Film grain — 3–5% noise overlay on dark backgrounds
Oversized portraits — instructors and training shots cropped close, desaturated to -15%, with a subtle flame gradient overlay on one edge
animations: Slow glow pulse on the flame accent (2–3s ease-in-out)
Mouse-parallax on hero globe imagery (8–12px max shift)
Text reveal on scroll — display type masks up from below, 400ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)
Hover on program cards: image desaturates → fully saturates + flame underline draws

colorRestraint: Per the user's explicit note: do NOT use strong colors site-wide. The flame, ice, and gold accents together should occupy less than ~8% of any page's total pixel real estate. The dark substrate and bone body text carry the weight. Think Netflix-original title sequence, not neon Vegas casino.
